compute.cost_note: CPU + en_core_web_sm; regex extraction, light per-turn tagging.

do_not_use_when: You want an author-identity, AI-provenance, or writing-quality verdict (the audit refuses all three)
The manuscript has little tagged dialogue (attribution is tag-heuristic, not coreference)
Target is under 2000 words (the length floor)

examples.description: JSON envelope with a baseline of other chapters

optional: --baseline-dir DIR (other chapters / a character's prior dialogue)

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

purpose: Per-character dialogue-voice profiling + cross-character divergence (Round 2 of pov_voice_profile, which profiles narration). Extracts quoted spans and tag-attributes them to speakers ("said X" / "X asked"); unattributed dialogue is bucketed separately and never force-attributed. Per character: contraction rate, mean turn length + variance, dialogue-tag verb diversity, vocative rate, interruption / trailing-punctuation rate, top function words + discourse markers. Reports a descriptive cross-character divergence matrix + a list of the lowest-divergence pairs — NOT a verdict. Refuses author-identity, AI-provenance, and quality inference.
use_when: Checking whether a multi-character draft keeps its characters voice-distinct in dialogue
Character-voice collapse is suspected (it surfaces in dialogue before narration)
You want a per-character spoken-register profile to pair with pov_voice_profile's narration layer
